free_text: Resonant and nonresonant capture of slow nrutrons in
Tm169(n,gamma)Tm170
High-flux beam reactor.
A sample of 192 g of powdered Tm2O3 inside an aluminum
7*7*1.5cm container with 0.8mm walls.
Flight path of the neutron beam is 21.7 m.
10cc GELI detector with a resolution of 12 keV
at 7 MeV used for gamma-ray detection.
Gamma intensities normalized to 12 high-energy gammas
from Sheline, et al., Phys.Rev.143, 857 (1966).
Gamma energies normalized to 6556.4 and 5945.0 keV
levels from same reference.
Relative efficiency of the detector obtained from
independent experiment on a CCl4 sample.
Statistical uncertainty.
Appears to be doublet.
Data taken from Table I in reference.
YH
At peak of resonance
Energies are recommended resonance energies taken from
BNL-325, 2nd Ed., Suppl.2 (1966)
Reactor spectrum.
